explanations.1: This sets the SHELL variable to bash, which tells Make to use bash as the command interpreter instead of the default sh.
explanations.3: This defines a color code (33 is yellow in ANSI terminal colors) that can be reused elsewhere in the Makefile.
explanations.5: .PHONY tells Make that 'help' is not a real file to create, just a target name for a command you want to run.
explanations.8: This uses awk (a text processing tool) to parse the Makefile and automatically generate a formatted help menu from comments marked with ##.
explanations.12: Lines starting with ##@ create section headers in the help output; the text after ##@ becomes a bold yellow section title.
explanations.19: This is another ##@ section header that will appear in the help menu to organize related commands.
explanations.30: This ##@ section header groups database-related commands together in the help output.
